evals.prompt:
I have a marketing brief for a summer coffee campaign. Here's the brief:

Campaign: Summer Refresh 2026
Brand: FreshBrew Coffee
Brand Colors: #2D5016 (forest green), #F5E6D3 (cream), #8B4513 (coffee brown)
Fonts: Playfair Display, Source Sans Pro
Tone: Warm, inviting, premium but approachable
Target Audience: Urban professionals 25-40
Required Formats: Instagram square (1:1)
Scenes:
1. Steaming coffee on a sunlit cafe table with pastries, morning light
2. Cold brew bottle on a picnic blanket in a park, summer afternoon
Headlines: "Refresh Your Ritual" / "Crafted for Your Best Moments"

Can you run the Brief2Campaign pipeline on this?
evals.expected_output: Agent should check environment (.env keys, dependencies), save the brief text, run 'python bria_marketing_agent.py generate --brief-text "..." --output output', wait for completion, read output/candidates.json, display candidate images inline using the Read tool, and ask the user which images to select.

evals.prompt: I need to run the marketing pipeline for our new energy drink. I have a brief in a DOCX file at ~/Documents/EnergyDrinkBrief.docx and product photos at ~/Desktop/can-front.png and ~/Desktop/can-side.png. I also want to use Bria ad template 1274 with brand ID 5678.
evals.expected_output: Agent should run 'python bria_marketing_agent.py generate --brief ~/Documents/EnergyDrinkBrief.docx --products ~/Desktop/can-front.png ~/Desktop/can-side.png --brand-id 5678 --templates 1274 --output output'. After generate completes, show candidate images, collect selection, then run 'python bria_marketing_agent.py finalize --candidates output/candidates.json --selected <ids> --brand-id 5678 --templates 1274'.

evals.prompt: Here are the generated images from the campaign. I want to use images 1 and 3 for the ads. The brand ID is 162 and template is 1274.
evals.expected_output: Agent should map 'images 1 and 3' to candidate IDs (e.g., edited_0 and edited_2), then run 'python bria_marketing_agent.py finalize --candidates output/candidates.json --selected edited_0 edited_2 --brand-id 162 --templates 1274'. After completion, read and display the ad images from output/4_ads/.
